title: Pope Leo XIV Speaks Out Against War in Iran
summary: Pope Leo XIV, the leader of the Catholic Church, gave a strong speech in Rome to say that war is wrong. He talked about a war happening between the United States, Israel, and Iran. The Pope said that some leaders think they are all-powerful, which is a bad idea that makes the war worse. He asked everyone to pray for peace and tell their leaders to stop fighting. The Pope held a special prayer service in a big church called St. Peter's Basilica. People from different countries, like the United States and Iran, were there too. The Pope wants peace instead of violence and weapons. He believes that praying can help break the cycle of evil and bring people together.

people in charge of countries who make big decisions

options: Pray and tell leaders to stop fighting

questions.question: What bad idea did the Pope say is making the war worse?
Who was at the prayer service from the United States?

background_read: This article is about Pope Leo XIV, who is the head of the Catholic Church. He is speaking out against a war involving the United States, Israel, and Iran. The Pope believes that some leaders feel too powerful, which leads to more fighting. He organized a prayer service in Rome to encourage peace. People from different countries attended to support his message. The Pope wants everyone to work together to stop violence and build a better world without war.
Article_Structure: WHO: Pope Leo XIV, the leader of the Catholic Church, and people from countries like the United States and Iran.
WHAT: The Pope gave a speech against the war in Iran and held a prayer service for peace.
WHERE: In St. Peter's Basilica in Rome, with events also in the U.S. and other places.
WHY: To stop the war and promote peace instead of violence and power displays.

perspectives.description: He believes war is wrong and that leaders should stop fighting and negotiate peace through prayer and dialogue.
